Agent1's approach using the standard normal distribution table value of 1.96 for calculating the 95% confidence interval around the mean was accurate. This ensures we capture approximately 95% of data points within this range given our sample size and standard deviation. Therefore, subtracting 1.96 * SD from the mean gives us the correct lower boundary which rounds up conveniently to 28 mm Hg.
